name: Depot
pricing_notes: Free: 500 min/mo; Growth $40/mo; Scale $200/mo; pay per build minute

performance.notes: Docker builds 10-50x faster vs standard Docker buildx; persistent cache across builds

agent_readiness.notes: Faster Docker builds via persistent BuildKit cache on remote builders. Drop-in `depot build` replacement for `docker buildx build`. Ephemeral builders with persistent cache. Multi-architecture (x86 + ARM) builds in parallel. GitHub Actions integration.

gotchas: Only valuable for Docker build workloads — not general CI
Per-minute pricing can be unpredictable for large builds
Requires network egress to Depot builders (data transfer)
Overkill for small teams with infrequent builds
best_when: Docker-heavy CI/CD where builds are a bottleneck (>5 min builds)
Multi-architecture builds (x86 + ARM) that take forever on emulation
CI/CD pipelines spending significant budget on build minutes
Agents that build Docker images as part of their workflows
avoid_when: Simple/fast Dockerfile builds not bottlenecked by build time
Environments where external build infrastructure isn't permitted
summary: Depot provides remote Docker build infrastructure with persistent BuildKit caches, accelerating Docker builds 10-50x vs standard GitHub Actions. Drop-in replacement: swap `docker buildx build` for `depot build`. Handles multi-architecture builds (x86 + ARM) in parallel without emulation. Best ROI for teams spending significant CI time on Docker builds.
